{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}} Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} at {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}} | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}}, Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the {{ill|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|ja|秩父多摩甲斐国立公園}}, the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and {{ill|Zuiheizan|ja|瑞牆山}}, in modern times many literati and artists—including {{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}, {{ill|Ibusuki Masuji|ja|井伏鱒二}}, {{ill|Nakayama Yoshihide|ja|中山義秀}}, and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.

In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Kawaguchiko|ja|河口湖}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}}, Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance {{ill|Iida Dakotsu|ja|飯田蛇笏}}. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters.
'''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|ja|1898th year }} – {{ill|1993|ja|1993rd year }}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen.
'''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|ja|1894th year }} – {{ill|1980|ja|1980年}}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}} Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} at {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}} | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}}, Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the {{ill|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|ja|秩父多摩甲斐国立公園}}, the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and {{ill|Zuiheizan|ja|瑞牆山}}, in modern times many literati and artists—including {{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}, {{ill|Ibusuki Masuji|ja|井伏鱒二}}, {{ill|Nakayama Yoshihide|ja|中山義秀}}, and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ({{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} – {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year }}) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Kawaguchiko|ja|河口湖}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}}, Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance {{ill|Iida Dakotsu|ja|飯田蛇笏}}. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters.
'''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|ja|1898th year }} – {{ill|1993|ja|1993rd year }}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen.
'''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|ja|1894th year }} – {{ill|1980|ja|1980th year }}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

'''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|ja|1898th year }} – {{ill|1993|ja|1993rd year }}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen.
'''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|ja|1894th year }} – {{ill|1980|ja|1980th year }}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>
== References ==

revision.text:
{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}} Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} at {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}} | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Hokuto City|ja|北杜市}}, Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the {{ill|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|ja|秩父多摩甲斐国立公園}}, the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and {{ill|Zuiheizan|ja|瑞牆山}}, in modern times many literati and artists—including {{ill|Takahama Kyoshi|ja|高浜虚子}}, {{ill|Ibusuki Masuji|ja|井伏鱒二}}, {{ill|Nakayama Yoshihide|ja|中山義秀}}, and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ({{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} – {{ill|1959|ja|1959th year }}) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Kawaguchiko|ja|河口湖}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}}, Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance {{ill|Iida Dakotsu|ja|飯田蛇笏}}. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters.
'''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|ja|1898th year }} – {{ill|1993|ja|1993rd year }}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen.
'''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|ja|1894th year }} – {{ill|1980|ja|1980th year }}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|ja|韮崎駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.hokuto-kanko.jp/guide/%e5%a2%97%e5%af%8c%e3%83%a9%e3%82%b8%e3%82%a6%e3%83%a0%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89%e5%b3%a1%e8%a6%b3%e5%85%89%e6%a1%88%e5%86%85%e6%89%80 増富 Radium Onsen Gorge Tourism Information Center] – Hokuto City Tourism Association, February 11, 2018 * [http://www.masutominoyu.com/ 増冨の湯]

50 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:増富温泉 up to rev 98721025
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 増富温泉 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} |コメント = |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[北杜市]]須玉町比志 |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[中央本線]][[韮崎駅]]より[[山梨交通]]バスで約60分 |泉質 = [[放射能泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''増富温泉'''（ますとみおんせん）は、[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]須玉町比志にある温泉である。'''増富ラジウム温泉'''とも呼ばれる。
== 泉質 == * [[放射能泉]] ** [[ラジウム]]含有量が非常に多い。1リットル中の含有量は12,300[[マッヘ]]との記録もある。 ** [[源泉]]温度は30℃近く、ぬる湯である。15分から30分と長めにつかる入浴法が一般的。 * 温泉概要（増冨の湯のデータを参考） ** 源泉泉質含二酸化炭素 - ナトリウム - 塩化物・炭酸水素塩泉（高張性中性低温泉） * 効能 ** 糖尿病・痛風・筋肉疲労・精神疲労・循環器障害・リュウマチ・アレルギー体質・内臓疲労アトピー性皮膚炎・更年期障害・肝機能障害・胆石症など
== 温泉街 == [[秩父多摩甲斐国立公園]]内、[[塩川 (山梨県)|塩川]]支流本谷川の侵食で渓谷が作られ、山間に8軒の[[旅館]]が存在する。[[共同浴場]]は無く、一般社団法人護持の里たまゆらが運営する[[日帰り入浴施設]] 「増富の湯」が1軒存在したものの、温泉施設老朽化に伴う点検等のため、2023年4月1日から当面の間休業している<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-3-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> 。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|高浜虚子]] 「増富」の地名は[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）から[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）まで存在していた[[増富村]]に由来する。[[武田信玄]]が[[金山]]開発中に発見した隠し湯であるという伝承をもつ。効能の高さから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]として栄えた。[[金峰山 (山梨県・長野県)|金峰山]]をや[[瑞牆山]]を望む景勝地で、近代には[[高浜虚子]]をはじめ、[[井伏鱒二]]や[[中山義秀]]、[[田中冬二]]をはじめ多くの文人や画人が訪れた。
[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）[[8月5日]]、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。[[2004年]]に発生した[[温泉偽装問題]]では、[[湧水]]利用であるにもかかわらず温泉利用と思わせる表記をしていた旅館があったと報じられた。
== 増富温泉を訪れた文学者 == '''高浜虚子'''（[[1874年]] - [[1959年]]）は[[俳人]]・[[小説家]]。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）6月に『[[國民新聞|国民新聞]]』紙で「[[富士山|富士]]を背景とする避暑的遊覧地」を募集し、山梨県では[[河口湖]]のほか日野原（北杜市長坂町）、増富が上位に選ばれた。虚子は紹介文を書くために山梨を訪れている。虚子は境川村（[[笛吹市]]境川町）出身の俳人で、大学時代に虚子と交流のあった[[飯田蛇笏]]（いいだ だこつ）の案内で増富温泉を訪れる。増富温泉では二泊し、『國民新聞』[[1917年]]（大正6年）7月9日・11日の号では歓迎の様子や宿の様子、渓谷の情景などを記している。また、飯田蛇笏も随筆「山の饗宴」においてこの時の様子を記しているほか、記念の[[揮毫]]や[[菅笠]]、書簡も残されている。
'''井伏鱒二'''（[[1898年]] - [[1993年]]）は昭和初年から戦後にかけて山梨県を数多く訪れ、山梨の文人とも交流している。山梨を舞台にした作品も多く手がけた。井伏は北巨摩（北杜市域）も数多く訪れているが、増富温泉も山宿として何度となく訪れ、[[1934年]]（昭和9年）に刊行された『田園記』に収録されている[[随筆]]「増富温泉場」では増富温泉について記している。
'''田中冬二'''（[[1894年]] - [[1980年]]）は銀行員の傍ら詩を書き、随筆も手がけた。山梨県では増富温泉のほか[[奈良田温泉]]（[[南巨摩郡]][[早川町]]）を訪れている。[[1955年]]（昭和30年）には、冬二の家族と画家の[[富田通雄]]の四人で増富温泉を訪れている。増富温泉を記した随筆に「山峡の湯」「増富ラジウム温泉」がある。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[中央本線]][[韮崎駅]]より[[山梨交通]]バスで約60分。 * 車：[[中央自動車道]][[須玉インターチェンジ]]より[[山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|県道604号]]・[[山梨県道601号増富若神子線|601号]]・[[山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|23号]]の増冨ラジウムライン<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=山梨全域道路愛称名対象路線及び愛称名 |publisher=山梨県 |page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>を通り車で約40分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.hokuto-kanko.jp/guide/%e5%a2%97%e5%af%8c%e3%83%a9%e3%82%b8%e3%82%a6%e3%83%a0%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89%e5%b3%a1%e8%a6%b3%e5%85%89%e6%a1%88%e5%86%85%e6%89%80 増富ラジウム温泉峡観光案内所] - 北杜市観光協会、2018年2月11日閲覧 * [http://www.masutominoyu.com/ 増冨の湯]
{{温泉}} {{coord|35|51|57|N|138|32|9|E|region:JP-19|display=title}}
{{Onsen-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[増富温泉]] to [[Masutomi Onsen]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 増富温泉 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} |コメント = |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[北杜市]]須玉町比志 |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[中央本線]][[韮崎駅]]より[[山梨交通]]バスで約60分 |泉質 = [[放射能泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''増富温泉'''（ますとみおんせん）は、[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]須玉町比志にある温泉である。'''増富ラジウム温泉'''とも呼ばれる。
== 泉質 == * [[放射能泉]] ** [[ラジウム]]含有量が非常に多い。1リットル中の含有量は12,300[[マッヘ]]との記録もある。 ** [[源泉]]温度は30℃近く、ぬる湯である。15分から30分と長めにつかる入浴法が一般的。 * 温泉概要（増冨の湯のデータを参考） ** 源泉泉質含二酸化炭素 - ナトリウム - 塩化物・炭酸水素塩泉（高張性中性低温泉） * 効能 ** 糖尿病・痛風・筋肉疲労・精神疲労・循環器障害・リュウマチ・アレルギー体質・内臓疲労アトピー性皮膚炎・更年期障害・肝機能障害・胆石症など
== 温泉街 == [[秩父多摩甲斐国立公園]]内、[[塩川 (山梨県)|塩川]]支流本谷川の侵食で渓谷が作られ、山間に8軒の[[旅館]]が存在する。[[共同浴場]]は無く、一般社団法人護持の里たまゆらが運営する[[日帰り入浴施設]] 「増富の湯」が1軒存在したものの、温泉施設老朽化に伴う点検等のため、2023年4月1日から当面の間休業している<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-3-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> 。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|高浜虚子]] 「増富」の地名は[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）から[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）まで存在していた[[増富村]]に由来する。[[武田信玄]]が[[金山]]開発中に発見した隠し湯であるという伝承をもつ。効能の高さから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]として栄えた。[[金峰山 (山梨県・長野県)|金峰山]]をや[[瑞牆山]]を望む景勝地で、近代には[[高浜虚子]]をはじめ、[[井伏鱒二]]や[[中山義秀]]、[[田中冬二]]をはじめ多くの文人や画人が訪れた。
[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）[[8月5日]]、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。[[2004年]]に発生した[[温泉偽装問題]]では、[[湧水]]利用であるにもかかわらず温泉利用と思わせる表記をしていた旅館があったと報じられた。

'''井伏鱒二'''（[[1898年]] - [[1993年]]）は昭和初年から戦後にかけて山梨県を数多く訪れ、山梨の文人とも交流している。山梨を舞台にした作品も多く手がけた。井伏は北巨摩（北杜市域）も数多く訪れているが、増富温泉も山宿として何度となく訪れ、[[1934年]]（昭和9年）に刊行された『田園記』に収録されている[[随筆]]「増富温泉場」では増富温泉について記している。
'''田中冬二'''（[[1894年]] - [[1980年]]）は銀行員の傍ら詩を書き、随筆も手がけた。山梨県では増富温泉のほか[[奈良田温泉]]（[[南巨摩郡]][[早川町]]）を訪れている。[[1955年]]（昭和30年）には、冬二の家族と画家の[[富田通雄]]の四人で増富温泉を訪れている。増富温泉を記した随筆に「山峡の湯」「増富ラジウム温泉」がある。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[中央本線]][[韮崎駅]]より[[山梨交通]]バスで約60分。 * 車：[[中央自動車道]][[須玉インターチェンジ]]より[[山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|県道604号]]・[[山梨県道601号増富若神子線|601号]]・[[山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|23号]]の増冨ラジウムライン<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=山梨全域道路愛称名対象路線及び愛称名 |publisher=山梨県 |page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>を通り車で約40分

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11429171]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]] Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] at [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]], Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the [[:en:Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park]], the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|qq=|1=Shio River (Yamanashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]]]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|qq=|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and [[:en:Mount Mizugaki|Zuiheizan]], in modern times many literati and artists—including [[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]], [[:en:Masuji Ibuse|Ibusuki Masuji]], [[:en:Gishū Nakayama|Nakayama Yoshihide]], and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|qq=|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|qq=|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|qq=|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|qq=|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|qq=|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|qq=|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429171]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]] Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] at [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]], Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the [[:en:Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park]], the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|qq=|1=Shio River (Yamanashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]]]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|qq=|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and [[:en:Mount Mizugaki|Zuiheizan]], in modern times many literati and artists—including [[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]], [[:en:Masuji Ibuse|Ibusuki Masuji]], [[:en:Gishū Nakayama|Nakayama Yoshihide]], and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|qq=|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|qq=|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|qq=|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|qq=|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|qq=|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|qq=|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429171]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]] Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] at [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]], Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the [[:en:Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park]], the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|1=Shio River (Yamanashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]]]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and [[:en:Mount Mizugaki|Zuiheizan]], in modern times many literati and artists—including [[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]], [[:en:Masuji Ibuse|Ibusuki Masuji]], [[:en:Gishū Nakayama|Nakayama Yoshihide]], and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429171]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]] Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] at [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]], Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the [[:en:Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park]], the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|1=Shio River (Yamanashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]]]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and [[:en:Mount Mizugaki|Zuiheizan]], in modern times many literati and artists—including [[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]], [[:en:Masuji Ibuse|Ibusuki Masuji]], [[:en:Gishū Nakayama|Nakayama Yoshihide]], and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429171]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]] Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] at [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]], Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the [[:en:Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park]], the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|1=Shio River (Yamanashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]]]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and [[:en:Mount Mizugaki|Zuiheizan]], in modern times many literati and artists—including [[:en:Kyoshi Takahama|Takahama Kyoshi]], [[:en:Masuji Ibuse|Ibusuki Masuji]], [[:en:Gishū Nakayama|Nakayama Yoshihide]], and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ([[:en:1874|1874]] – [[:en:1959|1959]]) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides [[:en:Lake Kawaguchi|Kawaguchiko]], Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]], Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance [[:en:Dakotsu Iida|Iida Dakotsu]]. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ([[:en:1898|1898]] – [[:en:1993|1993]]) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ([[:en:1894|1894]] – [[:en:1980|1980]]) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from [[:en:Nirasaki Station|Nirasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] via [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]]. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>
