<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]]
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}} Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} at {{ill|Nirasaki Station|en|Nirasaki Station|WD=Q8014441|lt=Nirasaki Station|1=Nirasaki Station}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kōtsū|en|Yamanashi Kōtsū|WD=Q8047883|lt=Yamanashi Kotsu|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū}} | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the {{ill|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|en|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|WD=Q656506|lt=Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|1=Chichibu Tama Kai National Park}}, the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|1=Q11428718|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Kyoshi Takahama|en|Kyoshi Takahama|WD=Q743767|lt=Takahama Kyoshi|1=Kyoshi Takahama}}]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and {{ill|Mount Mizugaki|en|Mount Mizugaki|WD=Q11573920|lt=Zuiheizan|1=Mount Mizugaki}}, in modern times many literati and artists—including {{ill|Kyoshi Takahama|en|Kyoshi Takahama|WD=Q743767|lt=Takahama Kyoshi|1=Kyoshi Takahama}}, {{ill|Masuji Ibuse|en|Masuji Ibuse|WD=Q705464|lt=Ibusuki Masuji|1=Masuji Ibuse}}, {{ill|Gishū Nakayama|en|Gishū Nakayama|WD=Q1963988|lt=Nakayama Yoshihide|1=Gishū Nakayama}}, and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ({{ill|1874|en|1874|WD=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}} – {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}}) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Q105261161|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Lake Kawaguchi|en|Lake Kawaguchi|WD=Q577372|lt=Kawaguchiko|1=Lake Kawaguchi}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} – {{ill|1993|en|1993|WD=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|en|1894|WD=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} – {{ill|1980|en|1980|WD=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|en|Nirasaki Station|WD=Q8014441|lt=Nirasaki Station|1=Nirasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kōtsū|en|Yamanashi Kōtsū|WD=Q8047883|lt=Yamanashi Kotsu|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Q105261161|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Lake Kawaguchi|en|Lake Kawaguchi|WD=Q577372|lt=Kawaguchiko|1=Lake Kawaguchi}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City|1=Fuefuki}}, Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance {{ill|Dakotsu Iida|en|Dakotsu Iida|WD=Q1657991|lt=Iida Dakotsu|1=Dakotsu Iida}}. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} – {{ill|1993|en|1993|WD=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|en|1894|WD=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} – {{ill|1980|en|1980|WD=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|en|Nirasaki Station|WD=Q8014441|lt=Nirasaki Station|1=Nirasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kōtsū|en|Yamanashi Kōtsū|WD=Q8047883|lt=Yamanashi Kotsu|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664609}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:増富温泉]]
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Masutomi Onsen (増富温泉) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=北杜市}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|qid=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}} Sutama Town, Hishi (須玉町比志) | Access = Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} at {{ill|Nirasaki Station|en|Nirasaki Station|qid=Q8014441|lt=Nirasaki Station|1=Nirasaki Station}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kōtsū|en|Yamanashi Kōtsū|qid=Q8047883|lt=Yamanashi Kotsu|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū}} | Spring Quality = {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Masutomi Onsen''' (ますとみおんせん), also known as '''Masutomi Radium Onsen''', is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|qid=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Sutama Town Hishi.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} ** It has an extremely high radium content; there are records indicating that the radium content reaches as high as 12,300 [[マッヘ]] per liter. ** The source water temperature is about 30°C, making it a lukewarm spring. A longer soaking time of 15 to 30 minutes is generally recommended. * Hot Spring Overview (based on data for “Masutomi no Yu”) ** The source water is classified as a carbon dioxide–containing, sodium, chloride, and bicarbonate hot spring (a hypertonic, neutral, low-temperature spring). * Efficacy ** Beneficial for conditions such as diabetes, gout, muscle fatigue, mental fatigue, circulatory disorders, rheumatism, allergic predispositions, visceral fatigue, atopic dermatitis, menopausal disorders, liver dysfunction, and cholelithiasis, among others.
== Hot Spring Town == Located within the {{ill|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|en|Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|qid=Q656506|lt=Chichibu Tama Kai National Park|1=Chichibu Tama Kai National Park}}, the erosion by the Honya River—a tributary of the {{ill|Shio River (Yamanashi)|ja|塩川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio River (Yamanashi)|1=Q11428718|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11428718}}—has carved out a valley where eight traditional {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|qid=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} are nestled in the mountains. There is no communal bath; however, one day-use bathing facility called “Masutomi no Yu” operated by the general incorporated association "Gochi no Sato Tamayura" existed. Due to aging facilities and the need for inspections, it has been temporarily closed since April 1, 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.masutominoyu.com/article.php?id=15 |title=「増富の湯」４月から休業のお知らせ|date=2023-03-10 |accessdate=2023-12-14}}</ref> == History == [[File:Kyoshi Takahama.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Kyoshi Takahama|en|Kyoshi Takahama|qid=Q743767|lt=Takahama Kyoshi|1=Kyoshi Takahama}}]] The name “Masutomi” derives from Masutomi Village, which existed from 1875 (Meiji 8) to 1959 (Showa 34). According to legend, it is a hidden hot spring discovered by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the development of a gold mine. Owing to its excellent efficacy, it flourished as a therapeutic bathing site. Situated in a scenic area with views of {{ill|Konpōzan|ja|金峰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpōzan|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10563986}} (in Yamanashi and Nagano) and {{ill|Mount Mizugaki|en|Mount Mizugaki|qid=Q11573920|lt=Zuiheizan|1=Mount Mizugaki}}, in modern times many literati and artists—including {{ill|Kyoshi Takahama|en|Kyoshi Takahama|qid=Q743767|lt=Takahama Kyoshi|1=Kyoshi Takahama}}, {{ill|Masuji Ibuse|en|Masuji Ibuse|qid=Q705464|lt=Ibusuki Masuji|1=Masuji Ibuse}}, {{ill|Gishū Nakayama|en|Gishū Nakayama|qid=Q1963988|lt=Nakayama Yoshihide|1=Gishū Nakayama}}, and {{ill|Tanaka Fuyuji|ja|田中冬二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Fuyuji|1=Fuyuji Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11575324}}—visited Masutomi Onsen. On August 5, 1965 (Showa 40), it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421080}}. In 2004, during the Onsen Adulteration Scandal, it was reported that some ryokan misrepresented their use of spring water to imply hot spring usage despite actually using ordinary spring water.
== Literary Figures Who Visited Masutomi Onsen == '''Takahama Kyoshi''' ({{ill|1874|en|1874|qid=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}} – {{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}}) was a haiku poet and novelist. In June 1917 (Taishō 6), he placed an advertisement in the {{ill|Kokumin Shimbun|ja|國民新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumin Shimbun|1=Kokumin Shinbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11422734}} calling for a summer leisure resort with {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|1=Q105261161|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105261161}} as a backdrop; in Yamanashi Prefecture, besides {{ill|Lake Kawaguchi|en|Lake Kawaguchi|qid=Q577372|lt=Kawaguchiko|1=Lake Kawaguchi}}, Hinohara (in Nagasaka-cho, Hokuto City) and Masutomi were Ranked highly. Kyoshi visited Yamanashi to write his introduction. Originally from Sakaigawa Village in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|qid=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City|1=Fuefuki}}, Kyoshi visited Masutomi Onsen under the guidance of his university acquaintance {{ill|Dakotsu Iida|en|Dakotsu Iida|qid=Q1657991|lt=Iida Dakotsu|1=Dakotsu Iida}}. He stayed for two nights at Masutomi Onsen; issues of the Kokumin Shimbun on July 9 and 11, 1917 described the warm welcome, the appearance of the inn, and the scenic valley. Iida Dakotsu also recorded the experience in his essay "Mountain Banquet," and left behind commemorative calligraphy, bamboo hats, and letters. '''Ibusuki Masuji''' ({{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} – {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}}) visited Yamanashi frequently from the early Shōwa period through the postwar era, interacting with local literati. He produced many works set in Yamanashi. Although he visited many parts of the northern Koma region (in Hokuto City), he also repeatedly visited Masutomi Onsen; his essay "Masutomi Onsen Scene," included in the 1934 publication "Den'enki" (Showa 9), describes Masutomi Onsen. '''Tanaka Fuyuji''' ({{ill|1894|en|1894|qid=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} – {{ill|1980|en|1980|qid=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}) was a banker who also wrote poetry and essays. In Yamanashi Prefecture, besides Masutomi Onsen, he visited {{ill|Narata Onsen|ja|奈良田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narata Onsen|1=Narada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11445134}} (in Minami-Koma District, Hayakawa Town). In 1955 (Showa 30), he visited Masutomi Onsen with his family and the painter {{ill|Tomita Michio|ja|富田通雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Michio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His essays on Masutomi Onsen include descriptions titled “Mountain Gorge Hot Spring” and “Masutomi Radium Onsen.”
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 60 minutes by bus from {{ill|Nirasaki Station|en|Nirasaki Station|qid=Q8014441|lt=Nirasaki Station|1=Nirasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} via {{ill|Yamanashi Kōtsū|en|Yamanashi Kōtsū|qid=Q8047883|lt=Yamanashi Kotsu|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū}}. * By Car: Approximately 40 minutes by car via the Masutomi Radium Line, which follows {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|ja|山梨県道604号小倉百観音線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 604 (Ogura Hyakkanon Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 604|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11469858}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|ja|山梨県道601号増富若神子線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 601 (Masutomi Wakakami Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11469855}}, and {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|ja|山梨県道23号韮崎増富線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Road 23 (Nirasaki-Masutomi Line)|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11469791}} from the {{ill|Sutama Interchange|ja|須玉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutama Interchange|1=Sutama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664609}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|qid=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/dourokanri/documents/yamanashikendouroaisyoumei.pdf |title=Roadway Nickname Targets and Nicknames for All of Yamanashi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}|page=1|accessdate=2018-04-08}}</ref>
